heading: Grants to Alaska to improve sanitation in rural and Native villages
label: 1263a Grants to Alaska to improve sanitation in rural and Native villages
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Safe Drinking Water Act Amendments of 1996, and not as part of the Federal Water Pollution Control Act which comprises this chapter.

children.text: the development and construction of public water systems and wastewater systems to improve the health and sanitation conditions in the villages; and
training, technical assistance, and educational programs relating to the operation and management of sanitation services in rural and Native villages.

children.heading: Federal share
children.label: b Federal share
children.text: The Federal share of the cost of the activities described in subsection (a) shall be 75 percent.

children.heading: Administrative expenses
children.label: c Administrative expenses
children.text: The State of Alaska may use an amount not to exceed 4 percent of any grant made available under this subsection for administrative expenses necessary to carry out the activities described in subsection (a).1So in original. Probably should be “section”.1

children.heading: Consultation with State of Alaska
children.label: d Consultation with State of Alaska
children.text: The Administrator shall consult with the State of Alaska on a method of prioritizing the allocation of grants under subsection (a) according to the needs of, and relative health and sanitation conditions in, each eligible village.
$40,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2022 through 2024;
